summary: Soundboard capture that’s generally clear, showcasing a scorching first set (Shakedown, Let It Grow, Loser) and a solid second half despite some early‑set muddiness.
review: The recording is a soundboard source with good overall clarity, though the opening of the first set sounds low‑mid and a few seconds of the Wheel intro are missing due to tracking glitches. Musically the show shines on the first‑set highlights – Shakedown, Let It Grow and Loser – while the second set stays strong but includes some over‑played moments (Fantasy/Hey Jude) and a lyrically‑challenged Bertha. Opinions vary, with many fans calling it a “hot” show and others finding it dull, giving the performance a mixed but generally positive vibe.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer star rating is ~3.4; recording quality is good (≈4) while show quality leans toward average‑good (≈3.3). Weighted combination yields a 3.5‑star overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp soundboard mix, but the early first‑set is low and slightly muddy; tracking issues cause a few seconds missing from The Wheel intro and occasional drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set burns hot with strong early songs and a memorable Let It Grow jam; second set is competent, delivering solid jams but lacking the same fire, ending with a warm Fantasy/Hey Jude/Lovelight sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for ripping on Shakedown and cutting loose, yet several reviewers note his tone sounds dull or ‘struggling’ during this night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Turned up nicely on the Let It Grow jam and contributed strong bass lines throughout; highlighted as a bright spot in an otherwise uneven show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Generally in good form; mentioned positively by Mooch and Antonjo for steady rhythm work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard) shines on Fantasy/Hey Jude and adds texture to the Let It Grow jam, though his synth can become a bit intrusive according to some listeners.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums/Space sections are described as favorable but not especially distinctive; drum intro leading into Truckin' is noted as standard.
